text.title: Microservices Observability Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real microservice observability rollouts.

antipattern.symptom: Tracing wired, but logs are unstructured plain text; during incident the operator can see the trace but cannot grep for log context.
antipattern.root-cause: Team rolled out one pillar at a time and stopped after tracing.
antipattern.fix: Make all three pillars a service ready-to-ship gate; pre-commit lints presence of structured logger + RED metrics + OTel tracer.

antipattern.symptom: Traces stop at service boundary; downstream spans appear as separate root traces.
antipattern.root-cause: Custom propagator on one service; W3C parser on the other; context lost.
antipattern.fix: Standardize on W3C propagator across all services; remove custom X-Trace-Id headers; use OTel auto-instrumentation where available.

antipattern.symptom: Logs are JSON but lack trace_id; operator must guess which log lines belong to which trace.
antipattern.root-cause: Logger not wired to OTel context; trace_id not in MDC / context.
antipattern.fix: Add OTel-MDC bridge (logback-otel, pino-opentelemetry-transport); every log gets trace_id automatically.

antipattern.symptom: Auditor flags trace data: `user.email = ruslan@faion.net` visible in third-party APM.
antipattern.root-cause: Auto-instrumentation captured request body or auth header without redaction.
antipattern.fix: Configure OTel span processor to redact PII fields; hash user identifiers; exclude request bodies for sensitive endpoints.
